medicines.name: Paracetamol
medicines.uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain such as headache, toothache, and fever reduction.
medicines.side_effects: Rare: skin rash, blood disorders, acute inflammation of the pancreas.
medicines.precautions: Avoid alcohol. Do not exceed 4g per day.
medicines.warnings: Severe liver damage may occur if you take more than recommended dose.

interactions.description: Prolonged regular use of paracetamol may enhance the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.

medicines.name: Crocin
medicines.uses: Fast relief from pain and fever.
medicines.precautions: Check for other paracetamol containing products.
medicines.warnings: Liver warning if taken in high doses.

medicines.name: Ibuprofen
medicines.uses: Anti-inflammatory painkiller for arthritis, back pain, and period pain.
medicines.precautions: Take with food. Inform doctor if you have asthma.
medicines.warnings: Increased risk of heart attack or stroke with long term use.

interactions.description: May increase risk of gastrointestinal bleeding.
May reduce the blood pressure-lowering effect.

medicines.name: Aspirin
medicines.uses: Prevents blood clots, heart attacks, and strokes.
medicines.precautions: Avoid in children under 16 due to Reye's syndrome.
medicines.warnings: High risk of stomach bleeding.

interactions.description: Increases risk of gastric ulcers and bleeding.

medicines.name: Amoxicillin
medicines.uses: Treatment of bacterial infections like chest infections and dental abscesses.
medicines.precautions: Complete the full course. Caution in patients with kidney problems.
medicines.warnings: Anaphylaxis possible in penicillin-allergic patients.

medicines.name: Atorvastatin
medicines.uses: Lowers 'bad' cholesterol (LDL) and prevents heart disease.
medicines.precautions: Avoid grapefruit juice. Monitor liver function.
medicines.warnings: Serious muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis) can occur.

medicines.name: Metformin
medicines.uses: First-line treatment for Type 2 Diabetes.
medicines.side_effects: Metallic taste, stomach upset, vitamin B12 deficiency.
medicines.precautions: Take with or after food. Check kidney function.
medicines.warnings: Lactic acidosis is a rare but life-threatening complication.

medicines.name: Lisinopril
medicines.uses: High blood pressure (hypertension) and heart failure treatment.
medicines.side_effects: Dizziness, dry cough, low blood pressure.
medicines.precautions: Monitor potassium levels. Avoid in pregnancy.
medicines.warnings: Angioedema (swelling of face/throat) is a critical risk.

interactions.description: Can impair kidney function and reduce blood pressure control.

medicines.name: Omeprazole
medicines.uses: Acidity, heartburn, and stomach ulcers.
medicines.precautions: Take 30-60 mins before food.
medicines.warnings: May mask symptoms of stomach cancer.

medicines.name: Cetirizine
medicines.uses: Antihistamine used to treat allergy symptoms.
medicines.precautions: Avoid driving if feeling sleepy.
medicines.warnings: Increases drowsiness if taken with alcohol.

medicines.name: Azithromycin
medicines.uses: Antibiotic for respiratory and skin infections.
medicines.precautions: Finish the full course.
medicines.warnings: Monitor for cardiac rhythm changes if you have heart history.

medicines.name: Amlodipine
medicines.uses: Treats high blood pressure and chest pain (angina).
medicines.precautions: Monitor ankle swelling.
medicines.warnings: Do not stop suddenly.

medicines.name: Doxycycline
medicines.uses: Treats acne and various bacterial infections.
medicines.precautions: Avoid direct sunlight. Do not take with dairy.
medicines.warnings: May discolor developing teeth in children.

medicines.name: Ecosprin-75
medicines.uses: Prevention of heart attack, stroke, and blood clots. Low dose aspirin is often used as a blood thinner.
medicines.precautions: Take after food. Avoid if you have asthma or bleeding disorders.
medicines.warnings: Consult doctor before surgery. Risk of stomach ulcers with long-term use.

interactions.description: May increase risk of stomach bleeding.

medicines.name: Combiflam
medicines.uses: Relief of muscular pain, joint pain, and fever reduction.
medicines.precautions: Do not take on empty stomach.
medicines.warnings: Avoid if you have active stomach ulcers.

medicines.name: Pantocid
medicines.uses: Acidity, heartburn, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
medicines.precautions: Take 30 minutes before breakfast.
medicines.warnings: Long-term use may cause bone fractures.

medicines.name: Digene
medicines.uses: Quick relief from acidity, gas, and stomach bloating.
medicines.precautions: Do not take for more than 2 weeks without medical advice.
medicines.warnings: Consult if you have kidney disease.

medicines.name: Vicks Action 500
medicines.uses: Relief from common cold, cough, and body aches.
medicines.precautions: Avoid other paracetamol products.
medicines.warnings: Do not use in children under 12.

medicines.name: Shelcal
medicines.uses: Calcium and Vitamin D supplement for bones and teeth.
medicines.precautions: Avoid overdose. Space 2 hours from other medications.
medicines.warnings: Hypercalcemia if overdosed.

medicines.name: Limcee
medicines.uses: Vitamin C deficiency, immunity booster, antioxidant.
medicines.precautions: Chew tablet. Avoid excessive doses.
medicines.warnings: High doses may cause kidney stones.

medicines.name: Monocef
medicines.uses: Severe bacterial infections including pneumonia, meningitis, and typhoid.
medicines.precautions: Monitor kidney function. Complete full course.
medicines.warnings: Risk of Clostridium difficile-associated diarrhea.

medicines.name: Cipla Actin
medicines.uses: Appetite stimulant and antihistamine for allergies.
medicines.precautions: Avoid alcohol. Do not drive.
medicines.warnings: Do not use in infants under 2 years.

medicines.name: Pan-D
medicines.uses: GERD, gastric reflux, acidity with nausea.
medicines.precautions: Take 30 minutes before food.
medicines.warnings: Not for long term use without medical advice.

medicines.name: Glycomet
medicines.uses: Type 2 Diabetes management. First-line therapy.
medicines.precautions: Take with meals. Monitor B12 levels.
medicines.warnings: Stop before surgery/contrast dye. Lactic acidosis risk.

medicines.name: Repace
medicines.uses: Hypertension and protection of kidneys in diabetic patients.
medicines.precautions: Monitor blood pressure and potassium.
medicines.warnings: Avoid in pregnancy — can harm fetus.

medicines.name: Telma
medicines.uses: High blood pressure treatment. Also used for cardiovascular risk reduction.
medicines.side_effects: Back pain, upper respiratory infection, dizziness.
medicines.precautions: Regular BP monitoring required.
medicines.warnings: Avoid in pregnancy. Monitor kidney function.

medicines.name: Thyronorm
medicines.uses: Hypothyroidism — underactive thyroid replacement therapy.
medicines.side_effects: Palpitations, weight loss, tremors if overdosed.
medicines.precautions: Take on empty stomach 30 min before food.
medicines.warnings: Do not self-adjust dose. Regular thyroid tests needed.

medicines.name: Dolo 650
medicines.uses: Fever and mild to moderate pain relief.
medicines.precautions: Do not take with other paracetamol-containing products.
medicines.warnings: Maximum 4 tablets per day.

medicines.name: Morphine
medicines.uses: Severe pain management, palliative care, post-surgical pain.
medicines.side_effects: Constipation, drowsiness, nausea, respiratory depression at high doses.
medicines.precautions: Highly addictive. Use only under strict medical supervision.
medicines.warnings: Risk of respiratory depression, especially if combined with CNS depressants.

medicines.name: Insulin Glargine
medicines.uses: Type 1 and Type 2 Diabetes — once-daily basal insulin.
medicines.side_effects: Hypoglycemia, injection site reactions, weight gain.
medicines.precautions: Monitor blood glucose regularly. Never mix with other insulins.
medicines.warnings: Risk of hypoglycemia if meal is skipped.

medicines.name: Insulin Regular
medicines.uses: Type 1 and Type 2 Diabetes — mealtime blood sugar control.
medicines.side_effects: Hypoglycemia, weight gain, injection site redness.
medicines.precautions: Inject 30 minutes before meals.
medicines.warnings: Incorrect dose can be fatal. Check expiry date.

medicines.name: Warfarin
medicines.uses: Prevention of blood clots, DVT, pulmonary embolism, and stroke in atrial fibrillation.
medicines.precautions: Regular INR blood tests essential. Consistent diet regarding Vitamin K.
medicines.warnings: Interaction with many foods and drugs. Life-threatening bleeding risk.

medicines.name: Atenolol
medicines.uses: Hypertension, angina, and heart rate control after myocardial infarction.
medicines.side_effects: Fatigue, cold extremities, dizziness, slow heart rate.
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly. Monitor heart rate.
medicines.warnings: Can worsen asthma and COPD. Not safe for severe heart block.

medicines.name: Propranolol
medicines.uses: Hypertension, anxiety, migraine prevention, heart rhythm disorders.
medicines.precautions: Do not stop suddenly. Avoid in asthma patients.
medicines.warnings: Can mask hypoglycemia symptoms in diabetics.

medicines.name: Furosemide
medicines.uses: Heart failure, edema (swelling), high blood pressure, kidney disorders.
medicines.side_effects: Frequent urination, electrolyte imbalance, dizziness, dehydration.
medicines.precautions: Monitor potassium levels. Take in morning to avoid nighttime urination.
medicines.warnings: Can cause profound electrolyte loss if overused.

medicines.name: Spironolactone
medicines.uses: Heart failure, hypertension, fluid retention, PCOS, acne.
medicines.precautions: Monitor potassium levels. Avoid potassium supplements.
medicines.warnings: Risk of hyperkalemia which can cause dangerous heart rhythm.

medicines.name: Digoxin
medicines.uses: Atrial fibrillation and heart failure — slows and strengthens heartbeat.
medicines.side_effects: Nausea, vision changes (yellow halos), irregular heartbeat.
medicines.precautions: Very narrow therapeutic window. Regular blood tests required.
medicines.warnings: Toxicity is serious — symptoms include vision changes and confusion.

medicines.name: Prednisolone
medicines.uses: Asthma, rheumatoid arthritis, allergic conditions, inflammatory bowel disease.
medicines.side_effects: Weight gain, insomnia, increased infection risk, high blood sugar, bone thinning.
medicines.precautions: Do not stop abruptly if taken long-term. Take with food.
medicines.warnings: Long-term use causes adrenal suppression and osteoporosis.

medicines.name: Dexamethasone
medicines.uses: Severe inflammation, allergic reactions, COVID-19 severe disease, brain swelling, cancer-related inflammation.
medicines.side_effects: Hyperglycemia, fluid retention, immunosuppression, mood changes.
medicines.precautions: Monitor blood sugar closely. Taper slowly.
medicines.warnings: Can mask signs of infection. Do not use in systemic fungal infections.

medicines.name: Ciprofloxacin
medicines.uses: Urinary tract infections, typhoid, respiratory infections, bone infections.
medicines.side_effects: Tendon damage, nausea, diarrhea, sun sensitivity.
medicines.precautions: Avoid dairy and antacids within 2 hours. Sun protection recommended.
medicines.warnings: Risk of tendon rupture, especially Achilles tendon.

medicines.name: Cotrimoxazole
medicines.uses: Urinary tract infections, Pneumocystis pneumonia in HIV, toxoplasmosis.
medicines.side_effects: Rash (can be serious), nausea, kidney damage.
medicines.precautions: Adequate hydration required. Monitor kidney function.
medicines.warnings: Can cause severe skin reactions (Steven-Johnson syndrome).

medicines.name: Hydroxychloroquine
medicines.uses: Rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, malaria prevention and treatment.
medicines.side_effects: Eye damage (retinopathy) with long-term use, nausea, dizziness.
medicines.precautions: Regular eye check-ups every 6 months.
medicines.warnings: Can cause serious cardiac arrhythmias at high doses.

medicines.name: Salbutamol
medicines.uses: Immediate relief (rescue inhaler) for asthma and COPD bronchospasm.
medicines.precautions: Use as rescue inhaler only. Overuse indicates poor control.
medicines.warnings: If you need more than 2 puffs > 2 days/week, see doctor.

medicines.name: Montelukast
medicines.uses: Asthma prevention, seasonal allergies, exercise-induced bronchospasm.
medicines.side_effects: Headache, abdominal pain, rare neuropsychiatric effects (mood changes).
medicines.precautions: Not for acute asthma attacks. Take in evening.
medicines.warnings: FDA warning about serious mental health side effects.

medicines.name: Rabeprazole
medicines.uses: GERD, stomach ulcers, H. pylori eradication (with antibiotics).
medicines.precautions: Take 30 minutes before food. Check for H. pylori if ulcers persist.
medicines.warnings: Long-term use may reduce magnesium and B12 levels.

medicines.name: Domperidone
medicines.uses: Nausea, vomiting, gastroparesis (slow stomach emptying).
medicines.precautions: Do not use in neonates or patients with liver problems.
medicines.warnings: Risk of cardiac arrhythmia at high doses or in vulnerable patients.

medicines.name: Ondansetron
medicines.uses: Prevention of chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting, post-operative nausea.
medicines.precautions: Monitor ECG in patients at risk of QT prolongation.
medicines.warnings: Avoid large doses as it can cause serotonin syndrome.

medicines.name: Alprazolam
medicines.uses: Anxiety disorders and panic disorder — short-term use only.
medicines.precautions: Do not drive. Avoid alcohol. Short-term use only.
medicines.warnings: High addiction potential. Cannot be stopped abruptly.

medicines.name: Escitalopram
medicines.uses: Depression, generalized anxiety disorder, social anxiety, OCD.
medicines.side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, sexual dysfunction, increased sweating.
medicines.precautions: Takes 2-4 weeks for full effect. Do not stop abruptly.
medicines.warnings: May increase suicidal thoughts in young adults initially.

medicines.name: Amitriptyline
medicines.uses: Depression, neuropathic pain, migraine prevention, insomnia.
medicines.side_effects: Dry mouth, drowsiness, constipation, weight gain, heart rhythm changes.
medicines.precautions: Take at bedtime. Avoid in elderly due to fall risk.
medicines.warnings: Overdose can be fatal. Cardiac toxicity risk.

medicines.name: Phenytoin
medicines.uses: Epilepsy — tonic-clonic and partial seizures.
medicines.side_effects: Gum overgrowth, acne, facial hair, ataxia, confusion.
medicines.precautions: Regular drug level monitoring required.
medicines.warnings: Zero-order kinetics: small dose increases can cause toxicity.

medicines.name: Tenofovir
medicines.uses: HIV infection treatment (as part of combination therapy). Hepatitis B.
medicines.side_effects: Kidney toxicity, bone density loss, nausea.
medicines.precautions: Monitor kidney function and bone density. Take with food.
medicines.warnings: Severe lactic acidosis if stopped abruptly in Hepatitis B patients.

medicines.precautions: Take with Vitamin B6 (Pyridoxine) to prevent neuropathy.
medicines.warnings: Liver function tests needed regularly. Alcohol worsens liver toxicity.

medicines.name: Carbamazepine

medicines.side_effects: Dizziness, rash, low sodium, liver effects.
medicines.precautions: Regular blood counts and liver function tests needed.
medicines.warnings: Severe skin reactions (Steven-Johnson syndrome) possible, especially in Asian populations — HLA-B*1502 testing recommended.

The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends that children up to age 16, in areas where drinking water contains less than optimal levels of fluoride, receive daily fluoride supplementation. The American Academy of Pediatrics recommend that infants and young children 6 months to 3 years of age, in areas where the drinking water contains less than 0.3 ppm of fluoride, and children 3-6 years of age, in areas where the drinking water contains 0.3 through 0.6 ppm of fluoride, receive 0.25 mg of supplemental fluoride daily which is provided in a dose of 1 mL of Tri-Vite Drops with Fluoride 0.25 mg (See Dosage and Administration ). Tri-Vite Drops with Fluoride 0.25 mg supply significant amounts of vitamins A, C and D to supplement the diet, and to help assure that nutritional deficiencies of these vitamins will not develop. medicines.precautions: PRECAUTIONS The suggested dose should not be exceeded since dental fluorosis may result from continued ingestion of large amounts of fluoride. When prescribing vitamin fluoride products: 1. Determine the fluoride content of the drinking water. 2. Make sure the child is not receiving significant amounts of fluoride from other medications and swallowed toothpaste. 3. Periodically check to make sure that the child does not develop significant dental fluorosis. Tri-Vite Drops with Fluoride 0.25 mg should be dispensed in the original plastic container, since contact with glass leads to instability and precipitation. (The amount of sodium fluoride in the 50 mL size is well below the maximum to be dispensed at one time according to recommendations of the American Dental Association.) medicines.name: Glipizide
medicines.uses: INDICATIONS AND USAGE Glipizide Tablets USP are indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus.
medicines.side_effects: ADVERSE REACTIONS In U.S. and foreign controlled studies, the frequency of serious adverse reactions reported was very low. Of 702 patients, 11.8% reported adverse reactions and in only 1.5% was glipizide discontinued. Hypoglycemia See PRECAUTIONS and OVERDOSAGE sections. Gastrointestinal Gastrointestinal disturbances are the most common reactions. Gastrointestinal complaints were reported with the following approximate incidence: nausea and diarrhea, one in seventy; constipation and gastralgia, one in one hundred. They appear to be dose-related and may disappear on division or reduction of dosage. Cholestatic jaundice may occur rarely with sulfonylureas: glipizide should be discontinued if this occurs. Dermatologic Allergic skin reactions including erythema, morbilliform or maculopapular eruptions, urticaria, pruritus, and eczema have been reported in about one in seventy patients. These may be transient and may disappear despite continued use of glipizide; if skin reactions persist,
medicines.precautions: PRECAUTIONS General Macrovascular Outcomes There have been no clinical studies establishing conclusive evidence of macrovascular risk reduction with glipizide or any other antidiabetic drug. Renal and Hepatic Disease The metabolism and excretion of glipizide may be slowed in patients with impaired renal and/or hepatic function. If hypoglycemia should occur in such patients, it may be prolonged and appropriate management should be instituted. Hypoglycemia All sulfonylurea drugs are capable of producing severe hypoglycemia. Proper patient selection, dosage, and instructions are important to avoid hypoglycemic episodes. Renal or hepatic insufficiency may cause elevated blood levels of glipizide and the latter may also diminish gluconeogenic capacity, both of which increase the risk of serious hypoglycemic reactions. Elderly, debilitated or malnourished patients, and those with adrenal or pituitary insufficiency, are particularly susceptible to the hypoglycemic action of glucose-lowering d
medicines.warnings: WARNINGS SPECIAL WARNING ON INCREASED RISK OF CARDIOVASCULAR MORTALITY The administration of oral hypoglycemic drugs has been reported to be associated with increased cardiovascular mortality as compared to treatment with diet alone or diet plus insulin. This warning is based on the study conducted by the University Group Diabetes Program (UGDP), a long-term prospective clinical trial designed to evaluate the effectiveness of glucose-lowering drugs in preventing or delaying vascular complications in patients with non-insulin-dependent diabetes. The study involved 823 patients who were randomly assigned to one of four treatment groups ( Diabetes , 19, supp. 2:747-830, 1970). UGDP reported that patients treated for 5 to 8 years with diet plus a fixed dose of tolbutamide (1.5 grams per day) had a rate of cardiovascular mortality approximately 2½ times that of patients treated with diet alone. diseases.name: Diabetes
diseases.explanation: A chronic condition where blood glucose levels are too high.
diseases.biology: The pancreas fails to produce insulin or cells become insulin-resistant, preventing glucose from entering cells for energy.

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.explanation: Persistently high pressure in the blood vessels.
diseases.biology: Arteries become narrowed or lose elasticity, forcing the heart to work harder to pump blood through the body.

diseases.name: Asthma
diseases.explanation: A respiratory condition marked by spasms in the bronchi of the lungs, causing difficulty in breathing.
diseases.biology: Airway inflammation and twitchiness lead to narrowing of the tubes that carry air into and out of the lungs.

diseases.name: Thyroid (Hypothyroidism)
diseases.explanation: The thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormone.
diseases.biology: The underactive thyroid slows down the body's metabolism leading to fatigue and weight gain.

diseases.name: Hyperacidity
diseases.explanation: Excess production of acid in the stomach.
diseases.biology: Lower esophageal sphincter (LES) malfunctions or stomach lining is irritated, allowing acid to reflux or cause ulcers.

diseases.name: Depression
diseases.explanation: A mental disorder characterized by persistent low mood, loss of interest, and impaired daily functioning.
diseases.biology: Disruption in serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine neurotransmitter systems in the prefrontal cortex and limbic system.

diseases.name: Anxiety
diseases.explanation: Excessive, uncontrollable worry that interferes with daily activities.
diseases.biology: Overactivation of the amygdala (fear center) and HPA stress axis, with dysregulation of GABA and serotonin.

diseases.name: Tuberculosis (TB)
diseases.explanation: A serious bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs.
diseases.biology: Mycobacterium tuberculosis infects macrophages and forms granulomas. It can remain latent for years.

diseases.name: HIV/AIDS
diseases.explanation: HIV destroys CD4 T-cells, progressively weakening the immune system until AIDS develops.
diseases.biology: HIV is a retrovirus that integrates its genome into host T-cells, making it impossible to clear without treatment.

diseases.name: Malaria
diseases.explanation: A life-threatening parasitic infection transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes.
diseases.biology: Plasmodium parasites infect red blood cells and liver cells, causing cyclical fever, chills, and organ damage.

diseases.name: Epilepsy
diseases.explanation: A neurological disorder causing recurrent unprovoked seizures due to abnormal brain electrical activity.
diseases.biology: Imbalance between excitatory (glutamate) and inhibitory (GABA) neurotransmission in the brain.

diseases.name: Heart Failure
diseases.explanation: The heart cannot pump enough blood to meet the body needs.
diseases.biology: Caused by damaged or weakened cardiac muscle (myocardium), leading to ventricular remodeling and neurohumoral activation.

diseases.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
diseases.explanation: An autoimmune disease causing chronic inflammation in joints.
diseases.biology: Autoreactive T-cells and B-cells target synovial tissue, releasing TNF-alpha and IL-6, destroying cartilage and bone.

diseases.name: COPD
diseases.explanation: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease — progressive lung disease causing breathing difficulty.
diseases.biology: Smoke/pollution destroys alveoli and causes chronic bronchitis, reducing lung elasticity (emphysema).

diseases.name: UTI (Urinary Tract Infection)
diseases.explanation: Bacterial infection of the urinary tract including bladder and kidneys.
diseases.biology: Bacteria (usually E. coli) colonize the urethra and ascend to the bladder, causing inflammation.

diseases.name: Atrial Fibrillation
diseases.explanation: Irregular, often rapid heart rate that can increase risk of stroke and heart failure.
diseases.biology: Disorganized electrical signals in the atria cause chaotic contractions instead of coordinated pumping.

diseases.name: Diabetes Mellitus Type 2
diseases.explanation: Chronic metabolic disorder characterized by high blood glucose.
diseases.biology: Insulin resistance or relative insulin deficiency.

diseases.name: Pneumonia
diseases.explanation: Infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs.
diseases.biology: Alveolar inflammation triggered by bacteria, viruses, or fungi.

diseases.name: Myocardial Infarction
diseases.explanation: Heart attack; blockage of blood flow to the heart muscle.
diseases.biology: Ischemia causing cellular death in the myocardium.
